title: hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. crestaensis
intro: Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. crestaensis, also known as the Crested Sea Hibiscus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the mallow family, Malvaceae. It is native to the Pacific Islands and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Polynesian medicine, where it is used to treat various ailments. The plant has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating wounds. Hibiscus tiliaceus subsp. crestaensis can be prepared in various herbal forms, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are used to treat a range of health issues.
